hook: You already know how to solve simple equations like $x^2 = 9$, but what happens when no real number satisfies the equation — like $x^2 = -1$? This unit gives you powerful new tools to crack open every quadratic equation, even the ones that seem impossible. Get ready to discover that the number system itself can be expanded to handle what you thought were unsolvable problems.
concept: Every quadratic equation has solutions — you just need the right tools and number system to find them. This unit builds from advanced solving techniques to a stunning idea called the 'Fundamental Theorem of Algebra': every polynomial equation has exactly as many solutions as its degree, once you allow complex numbers.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Solving Quadratics by Completing the Square
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to rewrite any quadratic expression as a perfect square, unlocking a reliable method to solve equations that can't be factored easily.
Master the quadratic formula $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ and use the discriminant $b^2 - 4ac$ to predict the number and type of solutions before you even solve.
You will learn to define the imaginary unit $i = \sqrt{-1}$ and represent complex numbers in the form $a + bi$, expanding the number system beyond the real line.
Apply addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to complex numbers, treating $i$ like a variable while remembering that $i^2 = -1$.
You will learn to solve quadratic equations whose discriminant is negative, expressing answers as complex conjugate pairs like $x = 3 \pm 2i$.
Explore the powerful theorem guaranteeing that every degree-$n$ polynomial has exactly $n$ solutions in the complex number system, tying together everything you've learned in this unit.
